text: You aren't standing still right now.
You're constantly falling, even on solid ground.
We usually think gravity is a mysterious force pulling things down.
But what if there's no 'pull' at all?
Massive objects, like planets or stars, don't just sit in space.
They actually bend and warp the very fabric of spacetime around them.
Think of it like a heavy ball placed on a stretched rubber sheet.
So, Earth doesn't orbit the Sun because the Sun 'pulls' it.
Instead, Earth is simply following the curves and valleys created by the Sun's immense mass in spacetime, like a marble rolling in a funnel.
So, gravity isn't a mysterious pulling force.
You're just following the path of least resistance through spacetime's warped geometry.
Send this to someone who loves mind-bending ideas and wants to see the universe differently!
